framework.name: Dual-Tag Transaction Intent Signaling
framework.description: Both protocols introduce a mandatory 'tag' field in the Signature-Input header with two discrete values: 'agent-browser-auth' for browsing/price discovery and 'agent-payer-auth' for actual purchase authorization. This allows merchants to apply different security policies based on transaction intent without inspecting request payloads.
framework.raw_quote: Both protocols build on Web Bot Auth by introducing a new tag that agents must supply in the Signature-Input header, which indicates whether the agent is browsing or purchasing. If the agent is browsing, the tag should be agent-browser-auth. If the agent is paying, the tag should be agent-payer-auth

source.title: Securing agentic commerce: helping AI Agents transact with Visa and Mastercard
